description: Moral Foundations Questionnaire (30 items) measuring 5 moral foundations using relevance and judgment items.
reference: Graham, J., Haidt, J., & Nosek, B. A. (2009). Liberals and conservatives rely on different sets of moral foundations.

items.text: Whether or not someone suffered emotionally
Whether or not some people were treated differently than others
Whether or not someone’s action showed love for their country
Whether or not someone showed a lack of respect for authority
Whether or not someone violated standards of purity and decency
Whether or not someone was cruel
Whether or not someone was denied their rights
Whether or not someone did something to betray their group
Whether or not someone conformed to traditions
Whether or not someone did something disgusting
Whether or not someone was harmed
Whether or not someone acted unfairly
Whether or not someone showed disloyalty
Whether or not someone challenged authority
Whether or not someone violated moral standards
Compassion for those who are suffering is the most crucial virtue.
When the government makes laws, the number one principle should be ensuring that everyone is treated fairly.
I am proud of my country’s history.
Respect for authority is something all children need to learn.
People should not do things that are disgusting, even if no one is harmed.
It is better to be nice than to be right.
Justice is the most important requirement for a society.
People should be loyal to their family members, even when they have done something wrong.
Men and women each have different roles to play in society.
I would call some acts wrong on the grounds that they are unnatural.
One of the worst things a person could do is hurt a defenseless animal.
I think it is morally wrong that rich children inherit a lot of money while poor children inherit nothing.
It is more important to be a team player than to express oneself.
If I were a soldier and disagreed with my commanding officer’s orders, I would obey anyway because that is my duty.
Chastity is an important and valuable virtue.
